author: Matt Pocock
text:
Quick PSA about Opus 4.7:

Anthropic have raised the default effort of Claude Code from medium to xhigh.

This will likely burn more tokens by default.

Switch off the defaults and experiment to see what effort level suits your work best.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
Quick PSA about Opus 4.7:

Anthropic have raised the default effort of Claude Code from medium to xhigh.

comments.author: Hariharan S
comments.text:
Oh god looks like a pattern
Make it high or xhigh
When new model is about to release make it medium. Ppl have to find it tucked away in release notes somewhere. When ppl complain of models getting nerfed “oh but based on user feedback we changed it” it is in release notes

comments.author: ben
comments.text: Question, are all the benchmarks they release based on a constant effort level?

comments.author: Dgrootonis
comments.text: So waste tokens trying to get the answer you’re looking for. Makes sense.

comments.author: BytePlus
comments.text: Easy setup. Access advanced coding models, generate, complete & refine code with AI.

comments.author: Boris Cherny
comments.text: We've raised rate limits for all subscribers to make up for the increased token usage. Enjoy!

comments.author: Pavel Buchta
comments.text:
xhigh is a scam.

high = cheaper and often slightly better in benchmarks

More thinking doesn’t always equal better results

comments.author: Jonathan Riche
comments.text: And its also confusing b/c when you open effort i see the Welcome to opus 4.7 xhigh but my efforts set to high (made me think i was on xhigh even tho defautl is high)

comments.author: Silvan Kübler
comments.text: or just dont use sloppy claud code ;)

comments.author: Thet Hmuu
comments.text: Matt, I always hoped you teach using Codex instead of Claude Code. Arguably that will improve the outcome a lot when combined with all your decent techniques.

comments.author: Dimitri Gilbert
comments.text: wow, they have done absolutely everything right for this release...

comments.author: Karim C
comments.text: been watching my usage since the update. definitely seeing 20-30% higher token burns on basic refactoring tasks. the output quality bump is real though

comments.author: Steve Korshakov
comments.text: jfc xhigh is so much worse than high simply because it starts to lean to distribution of training data instead of your code

comments.author: Peter Cruckshank
comments.text:
I'm always playing with the effort shifter. IDK how people can just set it and forget it. 
Like Opus high for git stuff

comments.author: Dennison
comments.text: This is what we get for complaining about it being dumb all the time. :-)

comments.author: Dave | Solo Dev
comments.text: noticed this yesterday, blew through a daily budget by noon before i caught it. dropped default to medium for refactors, kept xhigh for debugging gnarly stuff. good shout

comments.author: Everlier
comments.text: Very valid note. But there was also mention that reasoning effort is now more tightly correlated with verbosity. Granted how constrained they are on infrasturcture, it might be comparable to previous defaults.

comments.author: aira
comments.text: Changing the default effort is basically a pricing change. Users just feel it later when five prompts suddenly nuke their usage.

comments.author: Priyanka Pudi
comments.text: plan to burn more tokens !!

comments.author: andy
comments.text: are you complaining or something matt

comments.author: Mr Niewiadomski
comments.text: I don't see that switch in Claude Code Desktop anymore.

comments.author: Constantin
comments.text: Sneaky!
